_comment: Forge stack profile. PLACEHOLDER — neutral defaults, no stack assumed. The genesis interview (/forge-init) fills these fields and records a Stack ADR. Every skill and tool reads this file instead of hardcoding a stack. Field-by-field documentation: forge.config.schema.md. The leading underscore keys are documentation hints and may be removed once filled.

project._comment: Human-readable project name and one-line purpose. Filled by genesis.

stack._comment: All fields optional/nullable — a project may use none, some, or all.

conventions._comment: Casing and process conventions. Defaults below are Forge's own; a project may override.

requirementTiers._comment: Which spec weight genesis used. One of: lean | standard | full. Empty until chosen.

compliance._comment: Compliance regimes this project must honor (e.g. a privacy or certification standard). Empty by default — no compliance assumed.

docs._comment: Where the derived-docs generators write their output. Stack-neutral default.

traceability._comment: Knobs for the traceability generator. 'globs' are source patterns scanned for @requirement/@rule/@convention tags (** is recursive). 'tagAliases' maps a link kind to the tag keywords it accepts (defaults: requirement -> @requirement/@req, rule -> @rule/@businessRule, convention -> @convention/@conv). NOTE: 'examples/**/*' is a sensible default for adopters (their example code is real source worth tracing), but it is intentionally OMITTED here in Forge's OWN repo — Forge's only example is the domain-neutral golden example under examples/golden-example/, which is framework PROSE whose @requirement tags illustrate the mechanism and must not pollute Forge's own (empty) matrix. Genesis sets a project's real globs anyway; an adopter who keeps example code as source simply re-adds 'examples/**/*'.

_docsHooksExample._comment: OPTIONAL stack plugins run by forge-sync-docs AFTER the core generators. Leave docsHooks [] when a project has no stack-specific derived docs. Each hook is a shell command the project supplies. 'check' (optional) runs in --check mode; if absent, the writer command is re-run (idempotent). Copy an entry into docsHooks[] and edit.

examples.name: openapi
examples.command: <shell command that regenerates the API contract into docs.generatedDir>
examples.check: <shell command that fails if the committed contract is stale>

examples.name: client-generate
examples.command: <shell command that regenerates a typed API client from the contract>

criticalPaths._comment: Named flows that MUST be covered by tests (the Definition of Done gates these). Empty by default.

ci._comment: Continuous-integration profile. provider and commands filled by genesis/F8. strictValidation (default false) opts into a REQUIRED static-integrity check at /forge-init; when true, ci.commands.validate runs in CI as a gate that blocks merge unless the project is statically intact.
